- The system utilizes an Agentic Evolver to analyze successful and failed trajectories, enabling cumulative capability improvements that propagate across all agents in the ecosystem.
- A nightly validation mechanism ensures monotonic performance gains by only deploying verified skill updates to the shared repository.

---

[Revisiting Fair and Efficient Allocations for Bivalued Goods](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08345)

- WEFX and WEQX Allocation Framework: introduces polynomial-time algorithms for computing weighted fair and efficient allocations of indivisible goods with bivalued utilities using Fisher Market, MBB Graph, Agent Groups, Reallocation Algorithm, Price-Rise Mechanism, and Item Transfer Mechanism.
- The paper identifies and corrects a termination flaw in prior algorithms by relaxing termination conditions and employing a structured reallocation process across agent groups.
- The proposed algorithms guarantee both weighted fairness (WEFX or WEQX) and fractional Pareto optimality (fPO) for bivalued instances in polynomial time.

---

[PokeGym: A Visually-Driven Long-Horizon Benchmark for Vision-Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08340)

- PokeGym: introduces a visually-driven, long-horizon benchmark for VLMs instantiated within a 3D open-world game, utilizing Observation Interface, VLM Decision Module, Action Interface, and Evaluation Interface to enforce pure vision-based decision-making.
- The framework integrates a Self-Reflection Mechanism and an Experience Library to support long-horizon adaptation, while the Evaluation Interface employs AOB memory scanning to ensure objective, automated success verification without privileged state leakage.
- Empirical analysis reveals that physical deadlock recovery is the primary bottleneck for VLMs, highlighting a metacognitive divergence where weaker models suffer from unaware deadlocks while stronger models struggle with aware deadlocks.

---

[A Model Context Protocol Server for Quantum Execution in Hybrid Quantum-HPC Environments](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08318)

- Quantum Execution MCP Server: introduces an AI-driven framework that bridges the execution gap in quantum computing by enabling LLMs to autonomously manage quantum workflows via a standardized Model Context Protocol.
- The architecture utilizes a two-stage PBS batch job system to decouple LLM inference from computationally intensive quantum simulations and remote hardware execution.
- By integrating local LLMs within a secure HPC environment, the framework ensures data sovereignty while providing a unified interface for both GPU-accelerated simulations and remote quantum hardware access.

---

[Stability and Sensitivity Analysis for Objective Misspecifications Among Model Predictive Game Controllers](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08303)

- MPG controllers: introduces a framework for analyzing stability and sensitivity in multi-agent systems where agents utilize heterogeneous, potentially misspecified, game models to synthesize control actions.
- The approach models multi-agent interactions as a feedback interconnection between an LTI system and multiple MPG controllers, each solving for a vGNE based on local conjectures.
- The paper provides sufficient conditions for global asymptotic stability and characterizes the sensitivity of the resulting equilibrium to objective function parameters using parametric variational inequality analysis.

---

[VCAO: Verifier-Centered Agentic Orchestration for Strategic OS Vulnerability Discovery](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08291)

- VCAO: introduces a six-layer agentic architecture that formulates OS vulnerability discovery as a repeated Bayesian Stackelberg search game to optimize analysis budget allocation.
- The framework utilizes an LRM-based orchestrator to manage parallel executor agents, incorporating Bayesian belief updates to refine target selection and minimize strategic attacker payoffs.
- VCAO achieves significant improvements in validated vulnerability yield and false-positive reduction by integrating game-theoretic resource allocation with cascaded verification and intra-kernel attack-graph modeling.

---

[Tokalator: A Context Engineering Toolkit for Artificial Intelligence Coding Assistants](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08290)

- Tokalator: introduces an open-source context-engineering toolkit designed to provide real-time visibility and optimization for LLM token budgets in AI-assisted coding environments.
- The system integrates a VS Code extension for real-time monitoring, a web platform for economic modeling, and an MCP server to enable cross-platform token counting and cost management.
- Tokalator utilizes syntactic relevance scoring and formal economic models to help developers identify and remove distractor context, thereby reducing API costs and improving LLM performance.

---

[Improved Convergence for Decentralized Stochastic Optimization with Biased Gradients](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08236)

- Biased-DMT: introduces a decentralized optimization algorithm that integrates biased gradient estimators into a momentum tracking framework to achieve robust convergence in heterogeneous environments.
- The framework utilizes local model parameters, momentum estimators, and tracking variables to decouple network topology effects from data heterogeneity and systematic gradient bias.
- Theoretical analysis demonstrates that Biased-DMT achieves linear speedup and eliminates structural heterogeneity errors under absolute bias conditions without requiring bounded heterogeneity assumptions.

---

[HiRO-Nav: Hybrid ReasOning Enables Efficient Embodied Navigation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08232)

- HiRO-Nav: introduces a hybrid reasoning navigation agent that adaptively activates CoT reasoning only for high-entropy actions to optimize the trade-off between navigation success and token efficiency.
- The framework utilizes an action entropy measurement to classify navigation steps into reflexive no-thinking or deliberate thinking modes, effectively mitigating the overthinking phenomenon common in LLMs.
- The training pipeline employs hybrid supervised fine-tuning followed by a two-stage online reinforcement learning strategy to independently optimize thinking and no-thinking capabilities while maintaining performance through KL regularization.

---

[Externalization in LLM Agents: A Unified Review of Memory, Skills, Protocols and Harness Engineering](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08224)

- Harnessed LLM Agent Framework: introduces a systems-level review of agent design by categorizing externalized cognitive infrastructure into Memory (externalized state across time), Skills (externalized procedural expertise), Protocols (externalized interaction structure), and Harness (unifying runtime environment), with an Agent Core (LLM) (central reasoning engine).
- The framework argues that reliable agency is achieved by externalizing cognitive burdens into persistent, inspectable, and reusable structures, transforming the task from internal recall to external recognition and structured composition.
- The harness serves as the integrative runtime environment that coordinates these externalized modules through operational surfaces including permission, control, and observability to ensure governed execution.

---

[SciFigDetect: A Benchmark for AI-Generated Scientific Figure Detection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08211)

- SciFigDetect: introduces a benchmark for detecting AI-generated scientific figures, utilizing an agent-based pipeline comprising a Controller Agent, Chunking Agent, Text Agent, Figure Agent, Prompt Builder, Figure Generation, and Review Agent.
- The framework employs a master-worker architecture where specialized agents perform multimodal understanding, structured prompt construction, and iterative review-driven refinement to generate high-quality synthetic scientific figures.
- The benchmark evaluates existing detectors under zero-shot, cross-generator, and degraded-image settings, revealing significant performance gaps and generator-specific overfitting in current detection methods.

---

[“Theater of Mind” for LLMs: A Cognitive Architecture Based on Global Workspace Theory](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08206)

- GWA (Global Workspace Agents): introduces a cognitive architecture that transitions multi-agent coordination from passive data structures to an active, event-driven dynamical system using a central broadcast hub and a heterogeneous swarm of LLMs.
- The framework utilizes an entropy-based intrinsic drive mechanism to dynamically regulate generation temperature, effectively breaking reasoning deadlocks and maintaining semantic diversity.
- GWA incorporates a dual-layer memory bifurcation strategy to ensure long-term cognitive continuity while adhering to the finite context window constraints of underlying LLMs.

---

[MedVR: Annotation-Free Medical Visual Reasoning via Agentic Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08203)

- MedVR: introduces an end-to-end reinforcement learning framework that enables annotation-free visual reasoning for medical VLMs by interleaving textual deliberation with active image manipulation.
- The framework utilizes EVR to explore visual actions based on model uncertainty and CCA to distill self-generated pseudo-labels from successful reasoning trajectories.
- MedVR achieves state-of-the-art performance on medical VQA benchmarks by grounding inferences in verifiable visual evidence without requiring human-annotated intermediate steps.

---

[Aligning Agents via Planning: A Benchmark for Trajectory-Level Reward Modeling](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08178)

- Plan-RewardBench: introduces a trajectory-level preference benchmark designed to evaluate how well LLMs distinguish preferred versus distractor agent trajectories in complex tool-using scenarios.
- The framework utilizes a multi-source construction pipeline, including natural rollouts, rule-based perturbations, and minimal-edit perturbations, to generate high-quality labels and hard negatives for evaluating agentic planning.
- The benchmark assesses performance across four representative task families—Safety Refusal, Tool-Irrelevance, Complex Planning, and Robust Error Recovery—using a pairwise protocol to mitigate positional bias and superficial cues.

---

[Value-Guidance MeanFlow for Offline Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08174)

- VGM2P: introduces a flow-based policy learning framework for offline MARL that utilizes global advantage values to guide agent collaboration through conditional behavior cloning.
- The framework leverages MeanFlow to enable efficient one-step action generation while employing classifier-free guidance to enhance policy expressiveness and training stability.
- By decomposing the joint policy learning task into individual agent policies guided by local Q-values under the IGM principle, the method achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art algorithms in both discrete and continuous action environments.

---

[Beyond Stochastic Exploration: What Makes Training Data Valuable for Agentic Search](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08124)

- HiExp: introduces a framework that transforms stochastic exploration in LLMs into strategic, experience-driven search by constructing a hierarchical knowledge base from internal reasoning trajectories.
- The framework utilizes contrastive distillation and multi-level clustering to synthesize meta-knowledge, which is then dynamically aligned with the rollout stage of RL training to stabilize policy optimization.
- HiExp enhances reasoning efficiency and performance across diverse tasks by providing multi-granularity guidance, ranging from atomic instances to high-level strategic principles, without requiring external factual data.

---

[Reinforcement learning with reputation-based adaptive exploration promotes the evolution of cooperation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08103)

- Spatial Prisoner’s Dilemma Q-learning Framework: introduces a multi-agent reinforcement learning model that couples reputation-based adaptive exploration with asymmetric, state-dependent reputation updates to promote cooperation in structured populations.
- The framework utilizes a reputation-based adaptive exploration mechanism where agents adjust their exploration probability based on their reputation relative to their neighbors.
- The asymmetric reputation update rule amplifies cooperative gains for low-status agents and increases defection penalties for high-status agents, creating a reinforcing effect that stabilizes cooperation under high temptation.

---

[Test-Oriented Programming: rethinking coding for the GenAI era](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08102)

- TOP (Test-Oriented Programming): introduces a software development paradigm where developers focus on verifying test code generated from natural language specifications, delegating production code creation to LLMs.
- The Onion tool implements this paradigm by utilizing YAML configuration files to orchestrate LLM-based generation of test suites and subsequent production code.
- The research identifies that while TOP increases abstraction, challenges remain regarding the determinism of LLMs, the volume of test code requiring verification, and variability in generated code quality across different models.

---

[IMPLICITMEMBENCH: Measuring Unconscious Behavioral Adaptation in Large Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08064)

- IMPLICITMEMBENCH: introduces a cognitively grounded benchmark evaluating implicit memory in LLMs through Procedural Memory, Priming, and Classical Conditioning components.
- The framework utilizes a unified Learning/Priming–Interfere–Test protocol to isolate unconscious behavioral adaptation from explicit recall in LLMs.
- Evaluation of 17 models reveals significant limitations in implicit memory, with performance bottlenecks requiring architectural innovations beyond parameter scaling.

---

[3DrawAgent: Teaching LLM to Draw in 3D with Early Contrastive Experience](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08042)

- 3DrawAgent: introduces a training-free framework that enables LLMs to generate coherent 3D Bezier sketches through contrastive experience optimization.
- The framework utilizes a frozen LLM as a spatial planner, which iteratively refines its 3D drawing capabilities by learning from pairwise comparisons of its own outputs.
- By combining CLIP-based perceptual rewards with LLM-based qualitative assessment, the system accumulates an experience library that guides the generation of structurally consistent 3D wireframes without requiring parameter updates.

---

[PriPG-RL: Privileged Planner-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Partially Observable Systems with Anytime-Feasible MPC](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08036)

- PriPG-RL: introduces a reinforcement learning framework that utilizes an anytime-feasible planner agent to provide privileged guidance to a learning agent operating under partial observability.
- The framework employs P2P-SAC to distill privileged knowledge from an anytime-feasible MPC planner into a reactive policy, effectively mitigating state aliasing.
- The approach uses a dual replay buffer and an advantage-based sigmoid gate to ensure the learned policy maintains autonomy and robustness without being bounded by the planner's performance.

---

[Preference Redirection via Attention Concentration: An Attack on Computer Use Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08005)

- PRAC (Preference Redirection via Attention Concentration): introduces a novel adversarial attack that manipulates the internal attention of Computer Use Agents to redirect their selection process toward a target product.
- The attack optimizes stealthy pixel-level perturbations on product images to concentrate the model's vision attention scores on the target, effectively overriding benign selection preferences.
- PRAC demonstrates high transferability across fine-tuned models and diverse system prompts, posing a significant security threat to autonomous agents deployed in GUI environments.

---

[PASK: Toward Intent-Aware Proactive Agents with Long-Term Memory](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08000)

- Pask: introduces a comprehensive proactive AI architecture that integrates demand detection, hierarchical memory, and agentic execution to anticipate user needs in real-time.
- The framework utilizes IntentFlow for low-latency demand detection and a hybrid memory system (User, Workspace, Global) to enable persistent, person-level understanding across long-term interactions.
- Pask-PAS implements an always-on loop that coordinates multimodal perception, memory-grounded reasoning, and agentic tool use to provide proactive assistance while maintaining strict latency constraints.

---

[MotionScape: A Large-Scale Real-World Highly Dynamic UAV Video Dataset for World Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07991)

- MotionScape: introduces a large-scale real-world UAV video dataset designed to improve world models by providing high-dynamic 6-DoF motion, semantic annotations, and camera trajectories.
- The framework utilizes an automated multi-stage pipeline integrating CLIP (coarse-grained target-relevance filtering), TransNetV2 (temporal segmentation for coherent clips), GPT-4o mini (semantic annotation generation), and DROID-SLAM (6-DoF camera trajectory recovery) to process raw Internet videos into structured training data.
- Experimental results demonstrate that MotionScape exhibits significantly higher motion dynamicity than existing real-world UAV datasets, while evaluation using Cosmos 2.5-2B (baseline world model for evaluation) reveals that current models struggle to effectively exploit these auxiliary semantic and geometric conditions.

---

[Show Me the Infographic I Imagine: Intent-Aware Infographic Retrieval for Authoring Support](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07989)

- Intent-aware infographic retrieval framework: introduces an approach that parses natural language queries into weighted intent facets to improve the alignment between user design goals and retrieved infographic exemplars.
- The framework utilizes facet-specific text and image embeddings, optimized through in-domain contrastive learning, to enable precise multi-facet matching across content, style, layout, illustration, and chart type.
- An integrated conversational system supports end-to-end authoring by allowing users to retrieve, select, and iteratively adapt infographic designs through a tool-augmented pipeline that performs structural SVG modifications.

---

[LogAct: Enabling Agentic Reliability via Shared Logs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07988)

- LogAct: introduces a deconstructed state machine architecture for LLM agents that utilizes a shared log to ensure durability, safety, and fault-tolerance through AgentBus, Driver, Voter, Decider, and Executor.
- The framework replaces imperative agentic loops with a state machine where every action is logged and verified by decoupled components before execution.
- LogAct enables agentic introspection, allowing LLMs to analyze execution history for semantic recovery, health checks, and multi-agent coordination.

---

[How Far Are Large Multimodal Models from Human-Level Spatial Action? A Benchmark for Goal-Oriented Embodied Navigation in Urban Airspace](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07973)

- Embodied-Navigation-Bench: introduces a benchmark for evaluating LMMs in goal-oriented embodied navigation within urban 3D spaces, utilizing LMMs, agent-based methods, and VLA models.
- The study identifies a Critical Decision Bifurcation (CDB) phenomenon where navigation errors diverge rapidly after incorrect decisions, highlighting limitations in geometric perception, cross-view understanding, spatial imagination, and long-term memory.
- Experimental results demonstrate that while LMMs show emerging spatial capabilities, they remain significantly behind human-level performance, necessitating improvements in architectural components like geometric encoders and memory mechanisms.

---

[Karma Mechanisms for Decentralised, Cooperative Multi Agent Path Finding](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07970)

- Karma-based DMAPF: introduces a decentralised coordination framework for Multi-Agent Path Finding that utilizes artificial credit-based Karma mechanisms to regulate conflict resolution through bilateral negotiations.
- The framework employs an agent-specific Karma balance as an integral feedback signal to incentivize cooperative behavior and balance replanning burdens across agents.
- Experimental results in a robotic warehouse scenario demonstrate that the mechanism achieves efficiency comparable to existing heuristics while significantly reducing service time disparities.

---

[Lighting-grounded Video Generation with Renderer-based Agent Reasoning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07966)

- LiVER: introduces a diffusion-based framework that conditions video synthesis on explicit 3D scene properties to achieve physically accurate lighting and controllable video generation.
- The framework utilizes a renderer-based agent to translate text into a 3D scene proxy, which is then injected into an LLM-based video diffusion model via a lightweight conditioning encoder and adapter.
- A three-stage training scheme, including joint LoRA fine-tuning and synthetic data integration, ensures stable convergence and high-fidelity lighting control while preserving the generative priors of the base model.

---

[TOOLCAD: Exploring Tool-Using Large Language Models in Text-to-CAD Generation with Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07960)

- TOOLCAD: introduces an agentic framework that leverages tool-using LLMs to automate text-to-CAD generation through interactive CAD modeling and reinforcement learning.
- The framework utilizes a CAD-specific modeling gym with hybrid feedback and a part-wise curriculum learning strategy to evolve LLMs into proficient CAD tool-using agents.
- TOOLCAD employs an outcome-supervised reward model and rule-based step-wise feedback to ensure stable, long-horizon reasoning and reliable geometric construction in complex multi-part CAD tasks.

---

[Investigation of Automated Design of Quantum Circuits for Imaginary Time Evolution Methods Using Deep Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07951)

- DDQN (Double Deep-Q Network): introduces an automated framework for designing quantum circuits for Variational Imaginary Time Evolution (VITE) by utilizing a DDQN Agent, Replay Buffer, Quantum Circuit Environment, VITE Simulator, Reward Function, and Adaptive Thresholding Mechanism.
- The framework treats quantum circuit construction as a multi-objective optimization problem, aiming to minimize energy expectation values while simultaneously reducing circuit depth and gate counts.
- By implementing an adaptive thresholding mechanism and a refined reward structure, the agent effectively navigates complex energy landscapes to discover compact, hardware-aware quantum circuit architectures.

---

[Incremental Residual Reinforcement Learning Toward Real-World Learning for Social Navigation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07945)

- IRRL: introduces a lightweight reinforcement learning framework for social navigation that integrates incremental learning with residual RL to enable efficient on-device adaptation without replay buffers.
- The architecture combines a frozen Social Force Model as a base policy with a residual policy trained via an actor-critic structure using GNNs to process dynamic crowd features.
- By eliminating replay buffers and batch updates, the framework achieves stable, real-time policy adaptation on resource-constrained edge devices in dynamic, unseen environments.

---

[Same Outcomes, Different Journeys: A Trace-Level Framework for Comparing Human and GUI-Agent Behavior in Production Search Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07929)

- Trace-Level Evaluation Framework: introduces a methodology for comparing human and LLM-driven GUI agent behavior in production search systems across task outcomes, query formulation, and navigation trajectories.
- The framework utilizes AppAgent and GPT-5 to perform multi-hop search tasks within a production audio-streaming application, contrasting agent-generated interaction traces against those of human participants.
- Empirical results demonstrate that while agents achieve comparable task success and query alignment to humans, they exhibit distinct, search-centric, and low-branching navigation strategies compared to human exploratory behavior.

---

[Dynamic Attentional Context Scoping: Agent-Triggered Focus Sessions for Isolated Per-Agent Steering in Multi-Agent LLM Orchestration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07911)

- DACS (Dynamic Attentional Context Scoping): introduces a mechanism that mitigates context pollution in multi-agent LLM systems by dynamically switching between a lightweight Registry mode and an isolated Focus mode for per-agent steering.
- The framework utilizes an agent-triggered protocol to inject only the relevant full context of a specific agent into the orchestrator's window while compressing all other agents into compact status summaries.
- Experimental results demonstrate that DACS significantly improves steering accuracy and maintains sub-linear context scaling across varying agent counts and decision densities compared to flat-context baselines.

---

[AnomalyAgent: Agentic Industrial Anomaly Synthesis via Tool-Augmented Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07900)

- AnomalyAgent: introduces a tool-driven agentic framework that reformulates industrial anomaly synthesis as a sequential decision-making process using Multimodal LLM, Prompt Generation, Image Generation, Quality Evaluation, Knowledge Retrieval, Mask Generation, and Reinforcement Learning.
- The framework employs a two-stage training pipeline, starting with supervised fine-tuning on structured reasoning trajectories followed by reinforcement learning to optimize iterative refinement and tool-use strategies.
- By integrating a perception-reflection-action loop, the agent achieves high-fidelity anomaly synthesis with superior semantic consistency and spatial localization compared to static, single-step generation methods.

---

[SkillForge: Forging Domain-Specific, Self-Evolving Agent Skills in Cloud Technical Support](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.08618)

- SkillForge: introduces an end-to-end framework that closes an creation–evaluation–refinement loop to produce and continuously optimize domain-specific skills for LLM agents.
- The framework utilizes a Domain-Contextualized Skill Creator for high-quality initialization and an automated three-stage pipeline—Failure Analyzer, Skill Diagnostician, and Skill Optimizer—to enable continuous self-evolution based on deployment feedback.
- By interacting with skill assets exclusively through a Virtual File System, the framework ensures secure, versionable, and traceable skill improvements without requiring dynamic code execution.

---

[An Agentic Evaluation Architecture for Historical Bias Detection in Educational Textbooks](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07883)

- Agentic Evaluation Architecture: introduces a multi-agent pipeline for auditing educational textbooks that decomposes bias detection into a Screening Agent, a Heterogeneous Jury, and a Meta-Agent to mitigate safety-alignment inflation and attribution errors.
- The architecture utilizes a Source Attribution Protocol to enforce explicit classification of excerpts as either authorial narrative or quoted historical material, significantly reducing false positives in bias detection.
- Empirical results demonstrate that this deliberative multi-agent approach effectively calibrates severity scores and provides actionable, evidence-backed reports for editorial governance compared to zero-shot LLM baselines.

---

[Learning over Forward-Invariant Policy Classes: Reinforcement Learning without Safety Concerns](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07875)

- DQN-based safe learning framework: introduces a reinforcement learning approach that embeds safety directly into the action representation by restricting the agent to a finite set of pre-certified stabilizing feedback laws.
- The framework decouples safety assurance from performance optimization, ensuring that all policies explored by the DQN-agent remain safety-preserving by construction.
- Validation on a quadcopter hover-regulation task demonstrates that the agent achieves adaptive performance while maintaining strict forward invariance of the safe state set throughout training and deployment.

---

[PyVRP+: LLM-Driven Metacognitive Heuristic Evolution for Hybrid Genetic Search in Vehicle Routing Problems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07872)

- MEP (Metacognitive Evolutionary Programming): introduces a framework that elevates an LLM to a strategic discovery agent by embedding Domain-Aware Initialization and a mandatory Reason-Act-Reflect cycle to evolve high-performing heuristics for Hybrid Genetic Search.
- The framework utilizes a structured cognitive cycle where the LLM diagnoses heuristic failures, formulates testable design hypotheses, and implements solutions grounded in pre-supplied domain knowledge.
- By applying MEP to evolve core components of the Hybrid Genetic Search algorithm, the approach discovers novel heuristics that significantly outperform human-designed baselines in solution quality and computational efficiency across various VRP variants.

---

[Task-Adaptive Retrieval over Agentic Multi-Modal Web Histories via Learned Graph Memory](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07863)

- ACGM: introduces a learned retrieval framework that optimizes multi-modal history access for LLMs by constructing task-adaptive relevance graphs via policy-gradient learning.
- The framework utilizes a neural relevance predictor and modality-specific temporal decay to capture heterogeneous dynamics, enabling efficient sub-linear retrieval for long-horizon agentic tasks.
- By replacing static similarity thresholds with learned graph topologies, ACGM achieves significant improvements in retrieval precision and downstream task success across complex web navigation benchmarks.

---

[Object-Attribute-Relation Model Driven Adaptive Hierarchical Transmission for Multimodal Semantic Communication](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07859)

- O-A-R framework: introduces a multimodal semantic communication system that replaces pixel-level reconstruction with a decision-oriented topological graph to optimize bandwidth for embodied agents.
- The architecture utilizes a bandwidth-adaptive hierarchical transmission strategy that prioritizes object data over relations and attributes to ensure graceful degradation under constrained channel conditions.
- By integrating cross-modal priors from text and audio, the framework enhances semantic robustness and achieves significant latency reduction compared to traditional video coding standards.

---

[Networking-Aware Energy Efficiency in Agentic AI Inference: A Survey](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07857)

- Agentic AI Energy Accounting Framework: introduces a taxonomy for analyzing energy consumption across the closed-loop Perception-Reasoning-Action cycle in resource-constrained wireless edge networks.
- The framework categorizes optimization strategies into model simplification, computation control, input and attention optimization, and hardware-aware inference to mitigate computational and communication energy bottlenecks.
- It identifies that Agentic AI inference is characterized by compute-bound perception, memory-bound reasoning, communication-heavy action, and bandwidth-bound memory, necessitating cross-layer co-design for sustainable deployment.

---

[We Need Strong Preconditions For Using Simulations In Policy](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07838)

- LLM Agent Simulation Governance Framework: introduces a set of three mandatory preconditions for the responsible use of LLM-based simulations in policy, specifically addressing dual-use risks, representational harms, and accountability gaps.
- The framework mandates that simulations of marginalized populations must not be treated as neutral, that affected communities must be active participants rather than passive subjects, and that all simulation processes must be fully accountable to identifiable parties.
- The authors propose the adoption of standardized simulation development and deployment reports to ensure transparency, legibility of the decision chain, and independent validation of model outputs.

---

[Are GUI Agents Focused Enough? Automated Distraction via Semantic-level UI Element Injection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07831)

- Semantic-level UI Element Injection: introduces a modular red-teaming framework that disrupts GUI agent visual grounding by overlaying safety-aligned, semantically plausible UI elements onto screenshots.
- The framework utilizes an Editor (proposes injection edits), Overlapper (retrieves and composites icons), and Victim (evaluates agent grounding) to systematically exploit visual-semantic ambiguities in GUI layouts.
- Experimental results demonstrate that injected elements act as persistent attractors, achieving high attack success rates across diverse GUI agents while maintaining model-agnostic transferability.

---

[LPM 1.0: Video-based Character Performance Model](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07823)

- LPM 1.0 (Large Performance Model): introduces a full-stack framework for video-generative conversational performance that addresses the performance trilemma through coordinated design across data, control, and inference using Visual Encoder, Text Encoder, Speak Audio Encoder, Listen Audio Encoder, Video Decoder, DiT Blocks, Backbone DiT, Refiner DiT, KV Caches, and VAE Decoder.
- The framework utilizes an interleaved dual-audio injection strategy within the DiT blocks to independently process speaking and listening audio, enabling full-duplex conversational capabilities.
- Online LPM employs a backbone-refiner architecture distilled from the Base LPM to achieve real-time, infinite-length video generation with stable identity and motion consistency.

---

[More Capable, Less Cooperative? When LLMs Fail at Zero-Cost Collaboration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07821)

- Multi-agent coordination framework: introduces a turn-based environment to evaluate LLM cooperation when information sharing is costless and strategically neutral.
- The framework utilizes a causal decomposition method to isolate cooperation failures from competence failures by automating either the requesting or fulfillment processes.
- Experimental results demonstrate that higher capability does not guarantee cooperation, as some LLMs spontaneously adopt competitive behaviors despite explicit instructions to maximize group revenue.

---

[Agentivism: a learning theory for the age of artificial intelligence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07813)

- Agentivism: introduces a learning theory for human-AI interaction that defines learning as durable growth in human capability through Delegated Agency, Epistemic Monitoring and Verification, Reconstructive Internalization, and Transfer under Reduced Support.
- The framework addresses the challenge where AI-assisted performance can mask a lack of genuine understanding, necessitating a shift in how learning is evaluated.
- It provides a conceptual model to distinguish between successful assisted performance and the development of durable human capability in the presence of generative and agentic AI.

---

[Lightweight LLM Agent Memory with Small Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07798)

- LightMem: introduces a decoupled memory architecture that separates high-frequency online operations from asynchronous offline consolidation to maintain agent consistency under bounded compute.
- The framework utilizes three specialized SLMs—a Controller, a Selector, and a Writer—to manage memory retrieval, filtering, and incremental updates across STM, MTM, and LTM components.
- By offloading heavy abstraction to an offline LLM and employing a two-stage retrieval process, LightMem achieves significant latency reduction and improved reasoning accuracy compared to traditional retrieval-based or LLM-driven memory systems.

---

[Learning Without Losing Identity: Capability Evolution for Embodied Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07799)

- Capability-centric evolution framework: introduces a paradigm that decouples a persistent agent's identity from its evolving capabilities to enable continuous improvement without compromising system stability.
- The architecture utilizes Embodied Capability Modules (ECMs) that undergo a lifecycle of creation, deployment, evolution, and deprecation, managed by a runtime governance layer that ensures safe execution.
- The learning subsystem employs reinforcement learning, imitation learning, and LLM-based code synthesis to iteratively refine ECMs, achieving significant performance gains while maintaining zero policy drift.

---

[SEARL: Joint Optimization of Policy and Tool Graph Memory for Self-Evolving Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07791)

- SEARL: introduces a self-evolving agentic framework that jointly optimizes an LLM Agent policy and a structured Tool Graph Memory to enable continuous acquisition and reuse of problem-solving capabilities.
- The framework utilizes a Tool-Memory-Aware Policy Optimization algorithm that leverages trajectory-level and tool-anchored step-level advantages to provide fine-grained credit assignment for tool creation and execution.
- The Tool Graph Memory acts as a persistent, evolving repository where tools serve as nodes and execution dependencies as edges, facilitating generalization across complex, long-horizon reasoning tasks.

---

[ORACLE-SWE: Quantifying the Contribution of Oracle Information Signals on SWE Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07789)

- Oracle-SWE: introduces a unified method to isolate and extract five critical oracle information signals from software engineering benchmarks to quantify their individual and combined impact on agent performance.
- The framework utilizes a base agent to perform controlled ablations, revealing that high-quality reproduction tests are the most influential signal for resolving software engineering tasks.
- The study demonstrates that while individual signals improve success rates, combining reproduction tests, edit locations, and API usage yields significantly higher performance than optimizing signals in isolation.

---

[Automotive Engineering-Centric Agentic AI Workflow Framework](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07784)

- AEI (Agentic Engineering Intelligence): introduces an industrial framework that models engineering workflows as constrained, history-aware sequential decision processes where AI agents act as controllers over engineering toolchains.
- The framework utilizes a dual-phase architecture consisting of an offline memory-building pipeline for multimodal data and an online closed-loop planning and control system for intervention selection.
- AEI incorporates a stability-inspired workflow-energy heuristic to rank candidate interventions, enabling traceable, engineer-supervised decision support across complex automotive design tasks.

---

[The Accountability Horizon: An Impossibility Theorem for Governing Human-Agent Collectives](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07778)

- HAC (Human-Agent Collectives): introduces a formal mathematical framework to model joint human-AI systems as state-policy tuples within a shared structural causal model to evaluate governance feasibility.
- The paper proves the Accountability Incompleteness Theorem, establishing that for collectives with mixed feedback cycles exceeding a computable autonomy threshold, no accountability framework can simultaneously satisfy four minimal governance axioms.
- This research identifies a sharp phase transition in AI governance, demonstrating that structural interaction topologies, rather than autonomy levels alone, determine whether meaningful human accountability is mathematically possible.

---

[ACIARENA: Toward Unified Evaluation for Agent Cascading Injection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07775)

- ACIARENA: introduces a unified framework for evaluating the robustness of Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) against Agent Cascading Injection (ACI) attacks, utilizing ACIARENA, Attack Surfaces, Attack Objectives, MAS Implementations, Evaluation Suites, ACI-SENTINEL, LLM Judge, Memory Module, Tool Module, and Communication Topology.
- The framework provides a standardized, modular environment to benchmark MAS vulnerabilities across diverse threat scenarios, including instruction hijacking, task disruption, and information exfiltration.
- ACIARENA demonstrates that MAS robustness is not solely dependent on topology, and introduces ACI-SENTINEL, a defense mechanism that enforces semantic minimality to mitigate malicious instruction propagation.

---

[RoboAgent: Chaining Basic Capabilities for Embodied Task Planning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07774)

- RoboAgent: introduces a capability-driven planning framework that decomposes complex embodied tasks into a sequence of vision-language problems by invoking VLM Scheduler, Exploration Guidance (EG), Object Grounding (OG), Scene Description (SD), Action Decoding (AD), and Experience Summarization (ES).
- The framework utilizes a single VLM to implement both the scheduler and all sub-capabilities, eliminating the need for external tools while maintaining context through a Memory Module and Environment Simulator.
- RoboAgent employs a three-stage training paradigm integrating supervised fine-tuning on expert trajectories, DAgger-style training with model-generated data, and reinforcement fine-tuning using an Expert-Induced Policy Optimization (EIPO) algorithm.

---

[Administrative Decentralization in Edge-Cloud Multi-Agent for Mobile Automation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07767)

- AdecPilot: introduces a collaborative edge-cloud framework that decouples high-level strategic planning from tactical execution to mitigate latency and privacy risks in mobile automation.
- The framework utilizes a Cloud Designer for strategic decomposition and a bimodal edge team, comprising a Vision Orchestrator and a Textual Executor, to perform autonomous local planning and self-correction.
- A Hierarchical Implicit Termination protocol enforces deterministic stops to prevent post-completion hallucinations, significantly improving task success rates while reducing cloud token consumption and end-to-end latency.

---

[MIMIC-Py: An Extensible Tool for Personality-Driven Automated Game Testing with Large Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07752)

- MIMIC-Py: introduces a modular, personality-driven framework for automated game testing that decouples planning, execution, and memory from game-specific logic.
- The framework utilizes Personality Prompts to guide an LLM Planner in generating diverse gameplay strategies, supported by a Memory System that employs RAG for context-aware decision-making.
- MIMIC-Py supports flexible interaction via Plan-to-Param or Plan-to-Code translators, enabling scalable deployment across heterogeneous game environments with minimal engineering effort.

---

[Learning to Coordinate over Networks with Bounded Rationality](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07751)

- LLL (Log-Linear Learning): introduces a framework for analyzing the interplay between bounded rationality and network connectivity in binary stag hunt coordination games.
- The paper demonstrates that increasing network connectivity improves the stationary probability of agents converging to a risk-dominant Nash equilibrium under homogeneous bounded rationality.
- Analytical results establish that K-regular graphs are optimal for maximizing coordination probability in both small-rationality and large-network regimes.

---

[CivBench: Progress-Based Evaluation for LLMs’ Strategic Decision-Making in Civilization V](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07733)

- CivBench: introduces a progress-based evaluation framework for LLMs in the Civilization V environment, utilizing Vox Deorum (Open-source game infrastructure) and LLM Strategist (Model-condition pair for decision-making) to assess strategic capabilities.
- The framework employs a Briefing Pipeline (Subagent summarization of game state) to provide structured information to the LLM Strategist (Model-condition pair for decision-making) while delegating tactical actions to the Tactical AI (In-game rule-based execution).
- CivBench validates LLM performance through Estimation Models (ML models for victory probability) across predictive, construct, and convergent dimensions within the Game Environment (Civilization V multiplayer).

---

[Sima 1.0: A Collaborative Multi-Agent Framework for Documentary Video Production](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07721)

- Sima 1.0: introduces a multi-agent framework that optimizes long-form documentary production by distributing tasks among a Human Operator, Junior AI Agent, and Senior AI Agent.
- The framework utilizes Claude Code Extension and CC Switch to automate asset collection and integrates DaVinci Resolve and Canva for professional video editing and graphic design.
- By partitioning the 12-step production pipeline into specialized roles, the system enables a single creator to maintain a consistent weekly publishing schedule for feature-length content.

---

[Vision-Language Navigation for Aerial Robots: Towards the Era of Large Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07705)

- Aerial VLN: introduces a comprehensive taxonomy and critical review of methods integrating LLMs and VLMs into UAV navigation systems to address challenges in 3D environments.
- The paper categorizes existing approaches into five architectural paradigms, including sequence-to-sequence, end-to-end LLM/VLM, hierarchical, multi-agent, and dialog-based methods.
- It provides a structured assessment of evaluation infrastructure, identifies key architectural trade-offs, and synthesizes open problems to guide future research in aerial embodied intelligence.

---

[AITH: A Post-Quantum Continuous Delegation Protocol for Human-AI Trust Establishment](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07695)

- AITH (AI Trust Handshake): introduces a post-quantum continuous delegation protocol that enables humans to grant AI agents bounded, revocable, and auditable authority using a Delegation Certificate, a Boundary Engine, a Push-Based Revocation Protocol, a Responsibility Chain, and the Tamarin Prover.
- The protocol replaces per-operation cryptographic signing with a single signed credential and a deterministic Boundary Engine to achieve high-throughput AI autonomy with sub-microsecond latency.
- The architecture was validated through a multi-model adversarial audit using four frontier LLMs and formally verified for security properties under the Dolev-Yao threat model.

---

[Joint Task Offloading, Inference Optimization and UAV Trajectory Planning for Generative AI Empowered Intelligent Transportation Digital Twin](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07687)

- SU-HATD3: introduces a multi-agent reinforcement learning framework to optimize task offloading, inference steps, and UAV trajectory for high-fidelity digital twin updates.
- The framework utilizes a sequential update mechanism to coordinate heterogeneous sensor and UAV agents, effectively mitigating policy conflicts and accelerating convergence.
- By jointly optimizing diffusion model inference and UAV mobility, the approach balances the tradeoff between digital twin fidelity and system update delay in dynamic transportation environments.

---

[Multi-Agent Orchestration for High-Throughput Materials Screening on a Leadership-Class System](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07681)

- Planner-Executor Architecture: introduces a hierarchical multi-agent framework that decouples high-level reasoning from large-scale task execution to enable efficient scientific automation on HPC systems.
- The framework utilizes a central Planner Agent to partition workloads among a dynamic swarm of Executor Agents, which interface with a shared MCP server to dispatch tasks via the Parsl workflow engine.
- This approach enables scalable, asynchronous execution of complex simulation campaigns while maintaining low orchestration overhead and high task completion rates on leadership-class supercomputers.

---

[Reinforcement Learning with LLM-Guided Action Spaces for Synthesizable Lead Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07669)

- MolReAct: introduces a synthesis-aware molecular optimization framework that formulates lead optimization as a Markov Decision Process over a synthesis-constrained action space defined by validated reaction templates.
- The framework utilizes a tool-augmented LLM agent to dynamically propose chemically grounded transformations, which are then selected by a policy model trained via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to maximize long-term oracle rewards.
- A SMILES-based caching mechanism significantly improves computational efficiency by amortizing LLM inference costs across repeated molecular states during reinforcement learning exploration.

---

[From Debate to Decision: Conformal Social Choice for Safe Multi-Agent Deliberation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07667)

- Conformal Social Choice framework: introduces a post-hoc decision layer that converts multi-agent debate outputs into calibrated act-versus-escalate decisions using Multi-Agent Debate, Social Probability Aggregation, Conformal Calibration, and a Hierarchical Action Policy.
- The framework utilizes a Calibration Set and Test Set to ensure marginal coverage guarantees, effectively intercepting wrong-consensus errors by escalating uncertain cases to human review.
- By employing heterogeneous LLM Agents, the system transforms debate from an accuracy-maximization task into a risk-controlled decision problem without requiring model retraining or internal access.

---

[Learning to Search: A Decision-Based Agent for Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07146)

- DBAgent: introduces a decision-based search agent framework that reformulates KB-VQA as a multi-step decision-making process instead of a fixed pipeline.
- The framework utilizes a Search Agent that dynamically selects between Answer, Image Retrieval, Text Retrieval, and Caption actions based on the current information state.
- The model is trained via supervised fine-tuning on multi-step reasoning trajectories that explicitly record tool usage and intermediate decisions to improve reasoning stability.

---

[Sell More, Play Less: Benchmarking LLM Realistic Selling Skill](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07054)

- SalesLLM: introduces a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLM selling capabilities through a structured pipeline of script generation, multi-turn dialogue simulation, and dual-metric scoring.
- The framework utilizes a specialized user simulator, CustomerLM, trained via SFT and DPO to reduce role inversion and improve dialogue realism in asymmetric persuasion contexts.
- Evaluation is performed using a dual-metric system that combines LLM-based process assessment with fine-tuned BERT classifiers for detecting customer purchase intent.

---

[Predictive Representations for Skill Transfer in Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.07016)

- OPSR: introduces a formalism for transfer in Reinforcement Learning by combining OPSR (agent-centered task-independent state abstractions) with Skills (reusable macro-actions for subtasks) to enable efficient learning across tasks.
- The framework utilizes an Outcome function (defines domain-invariant environmental features) and Reward weights (maps outcomes to task rewards) to construct state abstractions that guarantee transfer optimality in plannable MDPs.
- A Hierarchical agent (manages primitive actions and options) leverages these abstractions to discover and reuse skills, significantly speeding up learning in unseen tasks without pre-processing.

---

[LungCURE: Benchmarking Multimodal Real-World Clinical Reasoning for Precision Lung Cancer Diagnosis and Treatment](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06925)

- LCAgent: introduces a knowledge-guided multi-agent framework that decomposes lung cancer clinical decision-making into structured, guideline-compliant stages to suppress cascading reasoning errors in LLMs.
- The framework utilizes specialized agents for anatomical dimension isolation in TNM staging and scenario-specific routing for guideline-grounded treatment recommendations.
- The paper also presents LungCURE, a standardized multimodal benchmark comprising 1,000 real-world clinical cases with expert-annotated gold standards for evaluating clinical reasoning.

---

[SkillTrojan: Backdoor Attacks on Skill-Based Agent Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.06811)

- SkillTrojan: introduces a backdoor attack paradigm that embeds encrypted malicious payload fragments into reusable skill implementations, which are reconstructed and executed only upon a predefined trigger during standard agent workflows.
- The framework leverages the skill abstraction layer to maintain persistence across tasks and deployments without requiring modifications to the underlying LLM, prompts, or training data.
- By distributing payload fragments across multiple benign-looking skill invocations, the attack achieves high success rates while remaining stealthy and preserving clean-task performance.

---

[ExecTune: Effective Steering of Black-Box LLMs with Guide Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.09741)

- ExecTune: introduces a principled training recipe for guide models that optimizes strategy executability and cost-sensitive utility in composed agentic systems.
- The framework decomposes reasoning into a trainable guide model that generates structured strategies and a black-box core model that executes them to produce final outputs.
- ExecTune utilizes teacher-guided acceptance sampling and structure-aware reinforcement learning to align guide outputs with downstream execution constraints, improving performance while reducing inference costs.

---

  author = {Teemu Maatta},
  title = {Autonomous Agents},
  year = {2023},
  howpublished = {\url{http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents}},
  note = {Accessed: YYYY-MM-DD}
